text:
The receptionist is a minor unnamed character who works for the State Military at Central City headquarters. She is typically seen working in the lobby near the telephones frequently used by Maes Hughes.

Appearance

).]]The receptionist has shoulder-length wavy hair with two long strands on either side of her face. Her hair is partially held up by a thick black hairclip (teal in the 2009 anime) on the back of her head. She is never shown in colour in the original manga, however her hair is shaded, indicating it is darker in colour. The 2003 anime depicts her with very dark brown hair, while the 2009 anime depicts her with dark dirty blonde hair with a greenish tint. Additionally, the manga depicts her with black eyes (indicating a dark eye colour) while the 2009 depicts them as being a light lavender colour. The 2003 anime only shows her from a distance, making it impossible to tell her eye colour. She's only ever seen wearing the standard State Military female uniform with an above-the-knee skirt and work-appropriate black pumps with thick heels.

History

The Receptionist has the same debut in the 2003 anime and original manga, that being when she lectures Maes Hughes after his phone call with Roy Mustang regarding new knowledge about the wanted murderer Scar, although her reason is different between versions. In the manga, the criticises him for using the phone to brag about his family, while in the 2003 anime she instead yells at him to get off from the desk he sits on top of. In the 2009 anime, she is seen during this same conversation, however she doesn't actually say anything, instead just sighing in exasperation at how enthusiastic he gets on the phone.

In the manga and 2009 anime, she is seen again as Maes Hughes makes his way to the phone in Central City's State Military Headquarters after being attacked by Lust. In the manga, she has a noticeably annoyed expression as she begins to scold him for being there to brag about his family again, while in the 2009 anime she smiles upon noticing him and playfully teases him, questioning who is going to hear about his family this time. In both cases, she begins to worry once she realises he's bleeding. In the 2009, she leans over her desk to get a better look at him as he makes his phone call, while in the manga, she leaves her desk to get him a first aid kit. In both cases, Maes changes his mind and hangs up the phone, apologising to the receptionist and leaving her concerned as he exits the building with an untreated wound. In the dubbed version, he specifically tells her to "forget I was here" as he leaves. In both the manga and anime, it is implied that Hughes (at least partially) decided not to use the telephone indoors due to the receptionist being nearby, not wanting harm to come her way.

The receptionist is last seen with Focker, crying over Hughes' death as she is questioned by Mustang over the circumstances leading up to his death.

Trivia

In the 2009 anime, during the scene where the receptionist explains the circumstances over Hughes' death, a stanchion barrier is added in front of the phone that Hughes attempted to call with. In the original manga, there is no such barrier.
Sheska largely takes over the Receptionist's role in the 2003 anime while also being greatly expanded on, with both characters being a female colleague of Maes Hughes who is frequently exasperated by Hughes' overwhelming love of his family, who also happens to witness his final moments leading up to his death.
Despite being a minor character and only having one scene in the 2003 anime where she appears in the background, she appears in both the Game Boy Advance game Fullmetal Alchemist: Stray Rondo and its sequel, Sonata of Memories (both based off the 2003 anime), as an NPC behind the front desk of the Central City headquarters, besides the same phones which Hughes frequently uses in the manga and anime. Additionally, while this NPC can be assumed to be the "real" receptionist character, her sprites and appearance are also reused for every single generic female State Military officer in the games.
Interestingly, in these games, she has dark blonde hair, matching more closely her appearance in the 2009 anime rather than her appearance in the 2003 anime (which has very dark brown hair).
